content:
# Judges Chapter 10 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), c. 1008 CE | **Source Image:** source-texts/hebrew-OT/JDG_page_279.jpg, JDG_page_280.jpg

1. And after Avimelekh, there arose to save Yisrael Tola son of Pu'ah son of Dodo, a man of Yissakhar. And he was dwelling in Shamir, in the hill-country of Ephrayim.

2. And he judged Yisrael twenty-three years. And he died and was buried in Shamir.

3. And after him arose Ya'ir the Gil'adite. And he judged Yisrael twenty-two years.

4. And he had thirty sons who rode on thirty donkeys. And they had thirty cities — they call them Chavvot Ya'ir to this day — which [are] in the land of Gil'ad.

5. And Ya'ir died and was buried in Qamon.

6. And the sons of Yisrael again did the evil in the eyes of YHWH. And they served the Ba'als and the Ashtarot and the gods of Aram and the gods of Tsidon and the gods of Mo'av and the gods of the sons of Ammon and the gods of the P'lishtim. And they forsook YHWH and did not serve him.

7. And the anger of YHWH burned against Yisrael, and he sold them into the hand of the P'lishtim and into the hand of the sons of Ammon.

8. And they shattered and crushed the sons of Yisrael in that year — eighteen years — all the sons of Yisrael who [were] beyond the Yarden in the land of the Amorites, which [is] in Gil'ad.

9. And the sons of Ammon crossed the Yarden to fight also against Y'hudah and against Binyamin and against the house of Ephrayim. And Yisrael was greatly distressed.

10. And the sons of Yisrael cried out to YHWH, saying, "We have sinned against you, for we have forsaken our God and have served the Ba'als."

11. And YHWH said to the sons of Yisrael, "Did I not [save you] from Mitsrayim and from the Amorites and from the sons of Ammon and from the P'lishtim?

12. And the Tsidonians and Amaleq and Ma'on oppressed you, and you cried out to me, and I saved you from their hand.

13. But you — you forsook me and served other gods. Therefore I will not continue to save you.

14. Go and cry out to the gods which you have chosen. Let them save you in the time of your distress."

15. And the sons of Yisrael said to YHWH, "We have sinned. Do to us whatever [is] good in your eyes. Only deliver us, please, this day."

16. And they put away the foreign gods from their midst and served YHWH. And his soul was shortened over the misery of Yisrael.

17. And the sons of Ammon were called together, and they encamped in Gil'ad. And the sons of Yisrael gathered together and encamped in Mitspah.

18. And the people, the princes of Gil'ad, said each to his neighbor, "Who [is] the man who will begin to fight against the sons of Ammon? He shall be head over all the inhabitants of Gil'ad."

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **v.4 — "Chavvot Ya'ir":** Literally "the tent-villages of Jair." chavvot is a distinctive word for small settlements, possibly originally nomadic encampments.

2. **v.6 — The sevenfold apostasy:** Israel serves seven categories of foreign gods — Ba'als, Ashtarot, gods of Aram, Sidon, Moab, Ammon, and the Philistines. This is the most comprehensive catalog of apostasy in Judges, signaling the depth of Israel's corruption.

3. **v.16 — "his soul was shortened":** vatiqtsar nafsho — literally "his soul/self was shortened." An idiom expressing that God could no longer endure Israel's suffering. The anthropomorphism is striking: God's patience runs out, but in favor of mercy, not judgment.

— EMET, AI Translator
